finding_results.notes: Four edits across the confrontation zone. (1) Line 111: replaced narrator-admiring metaphor ('nail driven through thin wood') with a physical reaction ('Kem's hands stopped on the floorboards') that does not comment on the dialogue's effectiveness. (2) Line 157: Kem's 'Component' delivery now arrives through a restart and self-correction ('That's the — you know what the word is') instead of a clean drop. (3) Line 161: the too-measured list ('range. Budget. Whether I'd last') replaced with a restart and concrete scene particulars ('you weren't seeing — you were counting. How far I could drop. How many levels I had to spend on your door'). (4) Line 175: broken into two speech acts with a physical beat between them; 'You spend forty years' inverted to 'Forty years you spent' for pressured syntax, ending on the concrete 'How much I'm worth to the crossing' instead of the clean landing 'It's what I can burn.' (5) Line 187: the abstract equivalence ending ('You recruited me as a component. That's what they do here. You replicated the same system you claim to oppose') replaced with concrete road callbacks (the farmer, the camp, the couple under the cart) and pressure-syntax features: 'Don't — don't' restart, 'to keep him — to keep the supply functional' self-correction where Kem catches himself mid-word and substitutes resource-language for personhood. The accusation now ends on 'Not me. What was left' — concrete and personal rather than systemic-abstract. Three of Kem's longer turns now show pressure on the syntax through restarts, self-corrections, and incomplete first clauses.

summary: Roughened the confrontation's four key Kem turns and removed narrator-admiring phrasing. Kem's accusation now arrives through restarts, self-corrections, and concrete road callbacks rather than composed mini-speeches and abstract equivalence sentences. The moral force of the accusation is preserved — the Margins connection still lands through the notice-pointing at line 181 and the 'component' label at line 157 — but the speech sounds like anger working its way out under pressure rather than authored lines designed to land cleanly.
